openmeter/billing/models/creditsapplied.purpose: Tiny value-object package modeling credits applied to a billing line. Defines CreditApplied (Amount/Description/CreditRealizationID) and the CreditsApplied slice, persisted as JSONB on detailed-line schemas and pre-tax in the totals model.

patterns.name: Validator + Clonable interface assertions
patterns.description: Compile-time interface checks via var _ blank assignments enforce that the type satisfies models.Validator and models.Clonable.
patterns.example: var _ models.Validator = (*CreditsApplied)(nil); var _ models.Clonable[CreditsApplied] = (*CreditsApplied)(nil)

patterns.name: Slice-level Validate delegates to element Validate
patterns.description: CreditsApplied.Validate() ranges items and returns the first item.Validate() error; CreditApplied.Validate() rejects non-positive Amount.
patterns.example: if !c.Amount.IsPositive() { return errors.New("amount must be positive") }

patterns.name: Value-semantics clone helpers
patterns.description: Clone() returns nil for empty slices and lo.Map copies elements; CloneWithAmount returns a copy with a new Amount (value receiver, no mutation of original).
patterns.example: func (c CreditApplied) CloneWithAmount(amount alpacadecimal.Decimal) CreditApplied { c.Amount = amount; return c }

patterns.name: Currency-aware summation
patterns.description: SumAmount rounds each item Amount with currencyx.Calculator.RoundToPrecision before adding, accumulating on alpacadecimal.Zero.
patterns.example: sum = sum.Add(currency.RoundToPrecision(item.Amount))

key_file_guides.role: Entire package: CreditApplied struct, CreditsApplied slice, Validate/Clone/SumAmount/CloneWithAmount.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Amount must be a positive alpacadecimal.Decimal — never compare decimals with ==; use alpaca methods. Clone returns nil (not empty slice) for len==0.

anti_patterns: Adding monetary fields without rounding through a currencyx.Calculator before summing.
Returning an empty non-nil slice from Clone() instead of nil — downstream goderive equality treats nil vs empty distinctly.
Mutating the receiver in CloneWithAmount/Clone (value receivers exist to keep these copy-only).

decisions.decision: Credits are stored as a standalone value-object slice rather than line fields.
decisions.rationale: Lets the same CreditsApplied JSONB blob be reused across detailed-line schemas and summed independently for pre-tax totals.

openmeter/billing/models/externalid.purpose: Generic, reusable Ent mixins and value-objects for storing external invoicing/payment/tax app IDs (e.g. Stripe invoice/line IDs) on invoice and line entities. Pairs schema field definitions with typed generic Create/Update/Map helpers.

patterns.name: Ent mixin + generic typed accessor pattern
patterns.description: Each entity gets a Mixin (LineMixin/InvoiceMixin) declaring Optional().Nillable() String fields, plus generic Creator/Updater/Getter interfaces and Create/Update/Map functions parameterized over the Ent builder type T.
patterns.example: func CreateLineExternalID[T LineExternalIDCreator[T]](creator LineExternalIDCreator[T], ids LineExternalIDs) T { ... }

patterns.name: EmptyableToPtr for optional persistence
patterns.description: Create/Update helpers use lo.EmptyableToPtr to turn empty strings into nil before SetNillable.../SetOrClear..., and lo.FromPtr when mapping back from DB.
patterns.example: creator.SetNillableInvoicingAppExternalID(lo.EmptyableToPtr(ids.Invoicing))

patterns.name: Create vs Update builder contracts differ
patterns.description: Creators use SetNillable* (insert), updaters use SetOrClear* (update can null out an existing value); pick the matching interface for the Ent operation.
patterns.example: SetOrClearInvoicingAppExternalID vs SetNillableInvoicingAppExternalID

patterns.name: Value-object Equal / nil-safe getter
patterns.description: LineExternalIDs.Equal compares by Invoicing; InvoiceExternalIDs.GetInvoicingOrEmpty is nil-safe on a pointer receiver.
patterns.example: func (i *InvoiceExternalIDs) GetInvoicingOrEmpty() string { if i == nil { return "" } ... }

key_file_guides.role: Ent mixins (LineMixin, InvoiceMixin) and the generic Create/Update/Map helpers + Creator/Updater/Getter interfaces.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Invoice has 3 app IDs (invoicing/payment/tax) but the InvoiceExternalIDCreator/Updater only wire invoicing+payment; tax_app_external_id is schema-only here. Keep Create/Update helper field sets in sync with the struct in model.go.

key_file_guides.role: InvoiceExternalIDs and LineExternalIDs value structs with omitempty JSON tags.
key_file_guides.watch_for: GetInvoicingOrEmpty is the only nil-safe accessor; InvoiceExternalIDs has no Equal (only Line does).

anti_patterns: Persisting empty strings instead of nil — always route optional IDs through lo.EmptyableToPtr.
Using a Creator interface for an update path (loses SetOrClear null-out semantics) or vice versa.
Adding a new external ID field to the struct without updating both the mixin Fields() and the generic Create/Update helpers.

decisions.decision: External IDs live in generic mixins reused across both invoice and line schemas.
decisions.rationale: Multiple Ent entities (invoices, detailed lines) need identical external-app-ID columns; generics over the builder type avoid per-entity boilerplate.

openmeter/billing/models/stddetailedline.purpose: Domain model + Ent mixin for standard invoice detailed lines (the Base aggregate). Composes the totals, externalid, and creditsapplied value-objects with line metadata (currency, service period, quantity, per-unit amount, category, payment term) and provides generic DB Create/FromDB mapping plus goderive-generated equality.

patterns.name: Composition over the shared value-object mixins
patterns.description: Base embeds models.ManagedResource and totals.Totals/externalid.LineExternalIDs/creditsapplied.CreditsApplied; the Ent Mixin() composes ResourceMixin + AnnotationsMixin + totals.Mixin via entutils.RecursiveMixin.
patterns.example: type Mixin struct { entutils.RecursiveMixin[mixinBase] }

patterns.name: Generic Creator/DBGetter mapping interfaces
patterns.description: Create[T Creator[T]] writes Base into any Ent builder satisfying Creator (which embeds externalid.LineExternalIDCreator + totals.Setter); FromDB[T DBGetter] reads it back, normalizing all times to UTC.
patterns.example: func Create[T Creator[T]](creator Creator[T], line Base) T

patterns.name: Aggregated Validate collecting errors with errors.Join
patterns.description: Base.Validate appends per-field wrapped errors (fmt.Errorf("category: %w", err)) into errs and returns errors.Join; supports functional ValidateOption (IgnoreQuantityChecks).
patterns.example: errs = append(errs, fmt.Errorf("service period: %w", err)); return errors.Join(errs...)

patterns.name: goderive equality, not reflect.DeepEqual
patterns.description: Base.Equal delegates to deriveEqualBase in derived.gen.go (regenerated via go:generate goderive); decimal/period fields compared with their Equal methods, not ==.
patterns.example: func (l Base) Equal(other Base) bool { return deriveEqualBase(&l, &other) }

patterns.name: Deprecated tax fields retained on the mixin
patterns.description: tax_config/tax_code_id/tax_behavior are .Deprecated(...) because detailed lines now inherit tax from the parent standard line; kept until the rollout migration completes.
patterns.example: field.JSON("tax_config", productcatalog.TaxConfig{}).Deprecated("detailed lines inherit tax configuration from their parent standard line")

patterns.name: UTC normalization at the DB boundary
patterns.description: Create and FromDB call .In(time.UTC) on every time.Time (service period, created/updated/deleted); CategoryRegular and InAdvancePaymentTerm are schema defaults.
patterns.example: SetServicePeriodStart(line.ServicePeriod.From.In(time.UTC))

patterns.name: Index-aware Compare ordering
patterns.description: Compare[T Comparable] orders by Index (nil sorts last), then CreatedAt, then ID — used to deterministically sort detailed lines.
patterns.example: if a.GetIndex() == nil && b.GetIndex() != nil { return 1 }

key_file_guides.role: Base aggregate, Category enum + Values()/Validate, ValidateOption, Clone, Equal, Compare/Comparable.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Clone only deep-copies CreditsApplied (rest are value types); Quantity may legitimately be negative-checked unless IgnoreQuantityChecks is passed (UBB persists quantity only at issue time).

key_file_guides.role: Ent schema fields/indexes/checks (currency varchar(3) immutable, numeric decimals, credits_applied JSONB, child_unique_reference_id non-empty check).
key_file_guides.watch_for: Editing fields requires make generate then atlas migrate diff. Several tax fields are .Deprecated — do not reuse them for new tax logic.

key_file_guides.role: DBGetter interface and FromDB[T] mapper; embeds externalid.LineExternalIDGetter + totals.TotalsGetter.
key_file_guides.watch_for: CreditsApplied comes from a *CreditsApplied pointer via lo.FromPtr; all times normalized to UTC.

key_file_guides.role: Creator[T] interface and Create[T] builder writer.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Must stay in sync with mixin Fields() and the Base struct; chains externalid.CreateLineExternalID and billingtotals.Set at the end.
Generated — DO NOT EDIT. Add fields to Base then run go generate; equality omits any field not regenerated.

anti_patterns: Editing derived.gen.go by hand instead of re-running goderive after changing Base.
Reaching for the deprecated tax_config/tax_code_id/tax_behavior fields — tax is inherited from the parent standard line.
Comparing Base or its decimal/period fields with == or reflect.DeepEqual instead of Equal/deriveEqualBase.
Returning on the first validation failure instead of collecting into errs and errors.Join.
Adding a Base field without updating mixin.go Fields(), create.go Creator, mapping.go DBGetter, and the goderive output together.

decisions.decision: Detailed-line persistence is expressed via generic Creator/DBGetter interfaces over Ent builders rather than concrete types.
decisions.rationale: Several Ent entities (billingstandardinvoicedetailedline, chargeflatfeerundetailedline, chargeusagebasedrundetailedline) share the same line shape; generics let one Create/FromDB serve all builders.
alpacadecimal and ClosedPeriod need semantic Equal comparison; reflect.DeepEqual would give wrong results on decimals.

code_examples.scenario: Persist a detailed line into any Ent builder via the generic Create helper
code_examples.code:
import (
  "github.com/openmeterio/openmeter/openmeter/billing/models/externalid"
  billingtotals "github.com/openmeterio/openmeter/openmeter/billing/models/totals"
)

func Create[T Creator[T]](creator Creator[T], line Base) T {
  create := creator.
    SetName(line.Name).
    SetCurrency(line.Currency).
    SetServicePeriodStart(line.ServicePeriod.From.In(time.UTC)).
    SetServicePeriodEnd(line.ServicePeriod.To.In(time.UTC)).
    SetQuantity(line.Quantity).
    SetPerUnitAmount(line.PerUnitAmount).
    SetCategory(line.Category).
    SetChildUniqueReferenceID(line.ChildUniqueReferenceID)
  create = externalid.CreateLineExternalID(create, line.ExternalIDs)
  create = billingtotals.Set(create, line.Totals)
  return create
}

openmeter/billing/models/totals.purpose: Money value-object aggregating the per-line/invoice monetary breakdown (Amount, ChargesTotal, DiscountsTotal, three tax totals, CreditsTotal, Total) plus an Ent mixin persisting each as a Postgres numeric. Provides decimal-safe arithmetic, validation, rounding, and DB Set/FromDB helpers reused across billing, charges, and rating.

patterns.name: Immutable value-semantics decimal arithmetic
patterns.description: Add/Sub/Neg/Sum/RoundToPrecision/CalculateTotal operate field-by-field with alpacadecimal methods and return new Totals (value receivers); never mutate in place.
patterns.example: res.Amount = res.Amount.Add(other.Amount)

patterns.name: Mixin Setter/Getter generic DB contract
patterns.description: totals.Mixin declares 8 numeric fields; Set[T Setter[T]] writes them onto any Ent builder and FromDB reads them via TotalsGetter — the canonical reuse hook for line/invoice schemas.
patterns.example: func Set[T Setter[T]](mut Setter[T], totals Totals) T { return mut.SetAmount(totals.Amount)... }

patterns.name: Sequential non-negative Validate
patterns.description: Validate returns the first negative-field error; ValidateTotalNonNegative is a cheaper Total-only check used where intermediate negatives are allowed.
patterns.example: if t.Amount.IsNegative() { return errors.New("amount is negative") }

patterns.name: CalculateTotal as the derived-total formula
patterns.description: Total = Amount + ChargesTotal + TaxesExclusiveTotal - DiscountsTotal - CreditsTotal (credits and discounts subtracted); use this rather than re-deriving inline.
patterns.example: alpacadecimal.Sum(t.Amount, t.ChargesTotal, t.TaxesExclusiveTotal, t.DiscountsTotal.Neg(), t.CreditsTotal.Neg())

patterns.name: Currency-aware rounding
patterns.description: RoundToPrecision rounds every field through a currencyx.Calculator before persistence/comparison.
patterns.example: t.Amount = calc.RoundToPrecision(t.Amount)

key_file_guides.role: Totals struct and all arithmetic/validation/rounding methods (Add, Sub, Neg, Sum, IsZero, Equal, RoundToPrecision, CalculateTotal, Validate, ValidateTotalNonNegative).
key_file_guides.watch_for: CreditsApplied/credits are pre-tax (CreditsTotal subtracted in CalculateTotal). Equal compares each decimal with .Equal — never ==. Any new field must be added to all 8-field methods consistently.

key_file_guides.role: Ent mixin (8 numeric fields) + Setter/Getter interfaces + Set/FromDB generic helpers.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Field list here, the Totals struct, and Set/FromDB must stay in lockstep; all fields are dialect.Postgres numeric.

key_file_guides.role: Round-trip rounding, Equal, and ValidateTotalNonNegative tests using USD calculator.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Asserts exact rounded strings (e.g. "10.01") — adding fields requires extending these assertions.

anti_patterns: Adding a Totals field without updating every method (Add/Sub/Neg/IsZero/Equal/RoundToPrecision) plus mixin Fields/Setter/Getter — silent drift loses the field.
Mutating a Totals receiver in place instead of returning a new value.
Comparing Totals or its decimal fields with == / reflect.DeepEqual instead of Equal.
Treating credits/discounts as additive in Total — CalculateTotal subtracts them.
Persisting or comparing un-rounded decimals; round via currencyx.Calculator first.

decisions.decision: Totals is a self-contained value-object with generic Setter/Getter DB hooks.
decisions.rationale: Both billing lines/invoices and the charges/rating subsystems persist identical monetary breakdowns; one mixin + generics avoids duplicating 8 numeric columns and arithmetic per entity.

decisions.decision: Credits are modeled pre-tax and subtracted in CalculateTotal.
decisions.rationale: Matches the invoice math where credits reduce the taxable base alongside discounts.
